elements.title: William H. Rieger
elements.contents: ... that William H. Rieger (pictured), a prominent American singer in the late 19th century, turned down an offered opera career in favor of remaining a concert tenor?

elements.title: a Harry Styles song
elements.contents: ... that a Harry Styles song was originally written as a Christmas song, but the concept was abandoned after the first two lines?

elements.title: Ruth Reinhold
elements.contents: ... that a plane flown by Ruth Reinhold was called "pilotless" because her height made it difficult for tower operators to see her in the cockpit?

elements.title: Cratoraricrus
elements.contents: ... that Cratoraricrus is a rare example of a fossilized centipede?

elements.title: Debbie Windett
elements.contents: ... that Debbie Windett, who went on to be one of the top-20 winningest coaches in U.S. high-school field hockey, was initially booed when she was hired?

elements.title: Mzimba District Hospital
elements.contents: ... that health workers supporting Mzimba District Hospital received bicycles to save walking up to 20 kilometres (12 mi) to visit patients?

elements.title: Shigeru Morita
elements.contents: ... that Shigeru Morita started oil painting at age 13?

elements.title: Terminator the Second
elements.contents: ... that Terminator the Second retells the story of Terminator 2: Judgment Day using only lines from the works of William Shakespeare?

elements.title: Panama women's national flag football team
elements.contents: ... that the Panama women's national flag football team won a gold medal after outscoring its opponents 349–7?
